title: calamagrostis contracta
intro: Calamagrostis contracta, also known as the finger grass or bent grass, is a type of perennial grass native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Calamagrostis contracta include reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. The herbal preparations of Calamagrostis contracta typically involve infusions or decoctions made from the leaves and stems of the plant, which can be consumed as a tea or used in topical applications for skin conditions.
